market_regime.trending_or_ranging: Trending — S&P 500 perpetual futures printing ATH signals broad equity momentum; Technology sector leading with +2.34% weekly gain; broader risk-on tone despite partial data gaps in sector scan.
market_regime.mean_reversion_note: Mean reversion strategies can remain active for equities. VIX at 16.7 is benign and supportive of normal position sizing. However, with indices near ATH, reversion trades should favour pullbacks-to-support rather than fading breakouts. Avoid chasing extended names.
market_regime.vix_interpretation: VIX 16.7 — NORMAL regime. Below the 20 warning threshold. Implied volatility is calm, suggesting the market is not pricing in near-term shock risk. Standard position sizing is appropriate; no need to cut size. If VIX spikes above 20 intra-week, reduce all equity exposure by 25%.
key_equity_macro_notes: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh sworn in — policy uncertainty is a latent risk; markets appear to be pricing a constructive transition for now.
US-Iran deal hopes driving crude oil lower — disinflationary, positive for consumer and tech sectors, negative for Energy.
Nasdaq 100 catalysts cited for the week — momentum in tech names is confirmed by XLK +2.34%.
VOO inflows topping $59B while SPY sees outflows — structural rotation toward passive large-cap, not a risk signal but worth monitoring for SPY liquidity.

top_opportunities.rationale: Technology sector (XLK +2.34%) is the only confirmed sector with positive weekly data, and QQQ is the liquid proxy for Nasdaq 100 mega-cap tech. top_opportunities.key_risk: Warsh Fed policy surprise or Iran deal collapse could cause intraday reversal.

Pullbacks to 20-day MA are mean-reversion entries.

crypto_outlook.sentiment_read: Fear & Greed Index at 25 (Extreme Fear), declining 3 points in the past 24 hours, signals capitulation sentiment that historically precedes mean-reversion bounces in BTC spot markets. While the fear reading is a contrarian buy signal, the deteriorating trend (-3 day-on-day) warrants patience — wait for stabilisation before sizing up.
crypto_outlook.dominance_read: BTC dominance at 58.25% confirms a BTC-led regime where altcoins are underperforming and capital is consolidating into Bitcoin. This is not an alt-favourable environment; ETH at 9.56% dominance is holding but alts further down the cap spectrum face ongoing outflows. Strategy should be BTC-first, ETH-second, minimal alt exposure.

crypto_risk_warnings.event: New Fed Chair Warsh — Policy Uncertainty
crypto_risk_warnings.detail: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed Chair. Unknown policy stance relative to crypto risk assets. Any hawkish signal from Warsh in first public remarks could trigger risk-off across crypto. Watch for any Fed communication this week.

Top Republicans publicly opposing the Iran deal. A deal collapse could spike crude, reverse the disinflationary narrative, and trigger a broad risk-off move including crypto. BTC could sell off 3-5% on a deal collapse headline.

Fear & Greed dropped 3 points in 24 hours to 25. If it continues declining toward 10-15 (Maximum Fear), mean-reversion setups fail and momentum to the downside accelerates. Set a hard rule: if Fear & Greed drops below 15, halt all new crypto longs and reduce existing exposure.

crypto_risk_warnings.detail: 58.25% BTC dominance confirms alts are underperforming. Positions in ADA, DOT, NEAR, and lower-cap alts carry disproportionate drawdown risk with limited upside capture relative to BTC this week.

macro_events_this_week.detail: Warsh was just sworn in. Any initial public statement, speech, or press appearance this week could move rates markets and risk assets significantly. This is the single largest macro unknown of the week.

Deal progress noted but Republicans opposing. Binary outcome risk — deal confirmed (crude down, equities up, crypto up) vs. deal collapse (crude spike, risk-off). Avoid Energy long positions; GLD hedge is prudent.
